research_series.series_name: Multi-Branch Differential Inference Templates for Symptoms,Phenotypes,Mechanisms,and Measurements

mediator.name: phenotype_uncertain_to_candidate_mechanism_cluster
mediator.pathway: symptom report is first mapped to phenotype uncertainty, then candidate mechanisms and needed measurements
mediator.mechanism: multimodal reasoning improves branch selection under uncertainty compared with single-modality inference

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not jump from symptom to mechanism. Use: symptom→phenotype uncertainty→candidate mechanisms→required measurements→confidence update→guardrails.

hypotheses.reason: Methodological reviews support multimodal fusion, Bayesian/probabilistic reasoning, and rule/graph systems for uncertainty-aware differential diagnosis.

evidence.summary: Multimodal AI/ML and Bayesian networks improve differential diagnosis under uncertainty, but most systems lack external validation, interpretability, workflow integration, and full-chain deterministic support.

key_sources.title: Methodological Approaches and Inference Templates for Differential Diagnosis
key_sources.relevant_finding: Reusable inference templates rarely achieve full-chain direct support; most serve as guidance tools.
key_sources.limitations: External validation and real-world deployment limited.

limitations: Knowledge graphs often lack real-world validation

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Multimodal AI models are ready as deterministic diagnostic engines
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Most current models lack robust external validation and real-world deployment evidence.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Intermediate node requiring symptom clarification before mechanism inference.
Recommended measurement required to update confidence in candidate mechanisms.

research_gaps: Need standardized evaluation for knowledge-graph/rule-based CDSS
Need external validation across symptom clusters

most_supported_patterns: Multimodal fusion improves diagnostic accuracy vs unimodal methods
Reusable templates are guidance rather than deterministic rules
recommended_next_queries: Final graph guardrails and validation protocol
summary.overall_assessment: Core methodological template layer for final system architecture.

works_cited.title: Methodological Approaches and Inference Templates for Differential Diagnosis, Clinical Reasoning, and Syndrome Phenotyping
